name: osint-mcp
description: Watch topics, brands, competitors, and sites across the public web. Raw signal in, your model decides what matters.
long_description: A small MCP server that watches the topics, brands, competitors, or sites you care about across the open web and hands back the raw hits. No LLM, no ranking, no dashboard. Sources: Hacker News, Reddit, DuckDuckGo, Stack Overflow, GitHub Issues, and any site's RSS or Atom feed (keyless except an optional GitHub token). Save targets (a search phrase or a site url), then fetch_updates returns recent mentions and, by default, only what is new since the last fetch. The relevance score is a hint, not a verdict. The reasoning stays with the client's model.

tools.name: list_sources
tools.description: List the sources it can search and which need a key.

tools.name: add_target
tools.description: Save a watch: a search phrase (name) and/or a site url to follow via RSS/Atom.

tools.name: remove_target
tools.description: Remove a saved watch by name.

tools.name: fetch_updates
tools.description: Fetch recent hits for a target, all targets, or an ad hoc name/url. new_only returns just what is new since last fetch.
